questions.question: What does `flex-basis` set?
questions.explanation: `flex-basis` sets the initial size of a flex item along the main axis before any growing or shrinking occurs.

questions.question: What is the default value of `flex-basis`?
questions.explanation: The default is `auto`, which uses the item's content size or its width/height property.

questions.question: In `flex-direction: row`, what dimension does `flex-basis` control?
questions.explanation: In row direction, flex-basis controls the width. In column direction, it controls the height.

questions.question: What does `flex-basis: 0` with `flex-grow: 1` achieve?
questions.explanation: `flex-basis: 0` ignores content size; with `flex-grow: 1`, all items grow equally from zero.

questions.question: What determines the wrapping point in `flex: 1 1 300px`?
questions.explanation: The flex-basis (300px) determines the ideal size and wrapping point in a flex-wrap layout.

questions.question: When flex-basis is set to auto and no explicit width is given, what size does the item use?
questions.explanation: With flex-basis: auto and no width, the item sizes to its content (shrink-to-fit).

questions.question: What happens when you set both width and flex-basis on a flex item?
questions.explanation: In a flex container, flex-basis takes precedence over width (or height in column direction).

questions.question: What does flex-basis: content do?
options: Sizes the item based on its content
questions.explanation: flex-basis: content sizes the item based on its content, similar to auto but without considering width.

questions.question: In flex-direction: column, what CSS property does flex-basis replace?
questions.explanation: In a column layout, flex-basis controls the height, effectively replacing the height property.

questions.question: What is the difference between flex-basis: 0 and flex-basis: 0px?
options: 0 is a keyword, 0px is a length
questions.explanation: In CSS, 0 is length-unitless. flex-basis: 0 and flex-basis: 0px are functionally identical.

questions.question: What does the shorthand flex: 1 set flex-basis to?
questions.explanation: flex: 1 expands to flex-grow: 1, flex-shrink: 1, flex-basis: 0. The basis is set to 0.

questions.question: What happens if flex-basis is larger than the container?
options: Items overflow or shrink depending on flex-shrink
questions.explanation: If total flex-basis exceeds container size, flex-shrink determines how items reduce, or they overflow.

questions.question: Can flex-basis use percentage values?
options: Yes, percentages are valid and relative to the container's main-axis size
questions.explanation: flex-basis accepts percentages, which are relative to the flex container's main axis size.

questions.question: How does flex-basis: auto differ from flex-basis: 0?
options: auto uses content/width size; 0 starts from zero regardless of content
0 uses content size; auto starts from zero
questions.explanation: auto respects the item's content and width properties; 0 ignores them and starts from zero.

questions.question: What is the effect of setting flex-basis to a very large value?
options: The item tries to take that much space, but grow/shrink and container limits apply
The item will always be that size
questions.explanation: flex-basis is the preferred size. The actual size depends on grow/shrink and container constraints.

questions.question: With flex-wrap: wrap, what role does flex-basis play?
options: It determines the minimum width before an item wraps to the next line
It has no effect with wrap
questions.explanation: With wrap enabled, flex-basis acts as an ideal width trigger for wrapping items to new lines.

questions.question: What does flex-basis: max-content do?
options: Sizes the item to the width needed by its content without wrapping
questions.explanation: max-content makes the item as wide as its content requires without any line wrapping.

questions.question: If you set flex-basis on a flex item and also set width, which wins?
questions.explanation: When an element is a flex item, flex-basis takes priority over the width property in row direction.

questions.question: What is the effect of flex-basis: fit-content?
options: Sizes the item to fit its content, clamped by min/max width
questions.explanation: fit-content sizes the item to its content, but within the bounds of min-width and max-width.

questions.question: What's the difference between setting width: 200px and flex-basis: 200px on a flex item?
options: flex-basis can be overridden by grow/shrink; width cannot be overridden by flex properties
width is ignored in flex containers
flex-basis is ignored in flex containers
questions.explanation: When flex-grow/shrink are active, flex-basis can change. A set width is more rigid but flex-basis still takes precedence as the starting point.
